description: The core vision statement ('Anyone who can build an interface can ship one... no design degree required') fails the swap test. It could equally describe a UI component library (like Tailwind UI), a website builder (like Squarespace), or an AI UI generator (like v0.dev). It describes the end result of 'shipping' but misses the product's unique mechanism: auditing and fixing an existing, post-MVP interface.
suggestion: Revise the statement to anchor on the product's actual space (automated evaluation/repair). For example: 'Every built interface gets a tireless, objective design QA that turns invisible defects into executable fixes.'
